# Rate-limited surface

Inventory of every endpoint that calls `checkRateLimit`. The table below is
**generated from the code** by `scripts/gen-rate-limits.mjs` — do not edit it by
hand; run the generator and commit. CI fails if it drifts (see MITIGATION_PLAN_V2.md §6).

Buckets are per-IP **and** (when an account key is passed) per-account; the
account bucket runs at **half** the IP `maxAttempts`, same window. Either bucket
tripping causes a 429 + `Retry-After`. `checkRateLimit` runs as the **first line**
of the handler, before any DB or crypto work.

<!-- BEGIN:rate-table (generated by scripts/gen-rate-limits.mjs — do not edit by hand) -->

| Endpoint | Action key | IP policy | Account bucket |
|---|---|---|---|
| `POST /api/v1/auth/forgot-password` | `forgot-password` | 5 / 60 min, 60 min lockout | email (3 / 60 min) |
| `POST /api/v1/auth/login` | `login` | 10 / 15 min, 15 min lockout | email (5 / 15 min) |
| `POST /api/v1/auth/mfa/send` | `mfa-send` | 3 / 10 min, 30 min lockout | userId (2 / 10 min) |
| `POST /api/v1/auth/mfa/verify` | `mfa-verify` | 10 / 10 min, 30 min lockout | userId (5 / 10 min) |
| `POST /api/v1/auth/register` | `register` | 10 / 60 min, 60 min lockout | — |
| `POST /api/v1/auth/resend-verification` | `resend-verify` | 3 / 60 min, 60 min lockout | userId (2 / 60 min) |

<!-- END:rate-table -->

> The table above is what I could confirm by reading the handler source. Two more
> endpoints — `POST /api/v1/auth/reset-password` (`reset-password`) and
> `POST /api/v1/auth/verify-email` (`email-verify`) — are rate-limited per the
> account-security skill but their handler files weren't inspected. **Run
> `node scripts/gen-rate-limits.mjs` once against the repo**: it will add those rows
> (or any others) with their exact values, and from then on CI keeps this in sync.

## What changed vs the previous hand-written table
- `register` was listed as `10 / 15 min` — the code is `10 / 60 min` (1-hour window/lockout).
- `forgot-password` was `5 / 15 min` with no account bucket — the code is `5 / 60 min`
  **and** passes `email`, so there's a per-email bucket (`3 / 60 min`).
- `register`'s account bucket was listed as `email` — the handler passes **no** account
  key, so there is none (`—`).
- `resend-verification` (`resend-verify`) was missing entirely.

## Dependencies / caveats
- IP resolution uses `getRequestIP(event, { xForwardedFor: true })`. **Caddy must
  overwrite `X-Forwarded-For`** on ingress, or the leftmost value is spoofable to
  rotate past the per-IP bucket. (Same trust-the-proxy requirement as the CSRF
  `X-Forwarded-Host` pin.)
- Never use a shared fallback bucket for unknown IPs — log and skip instead, or a
  single missing header locks out everyone.
- `Retry-After` is an HTTP-date produced from a `Date`; the service returns a
  `Date`, not delta-seconds.
